title: ranunculus dissectus subsp. napellifolius
intro: Ranunculus dissectus subsp. napellifolius, commonly known as lesser celandine or pilewort, is a perennial flowering plant with yellow, three-petaled flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The health benefits of lesser celandine include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Lesser celandine can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues and promote overall well-being.
